etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "grim" from Old English "grimm" (fierce, cruel)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Fierce cruel; having stern forbidding and grim sternness supported by softening awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Fierce cruel → stern forbidding → contemporary grim sternness and grim forbidding that benefits from gentleness growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Fierce cruel → stern forbidding → grim sternness → gentleness understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having grim sternness and grim forbidding; experiencing stern forbidding and grim grimness calling for softening gentleness
